feed.title: Counsel.day · The Journal
feed.subtitle: Long-form essays on the questions a single conversation cannot resolve. Written by James Graham, founder of Counsel.day.

entry.title: The Tuesday/Thursday problem
entry.summary: On big questions you have pro days and con days, and the difference between Tuesday and Thursday is rarely new information. It is mood, weather, fatigue, the last conversation. A single conversation can land on the wrong evening. The 2,100-word essay that opens the Journal.

entry.title: Why the seal has to be in the database, not in the settings (forthcoming)
entry.summary: A scheduled essay for June 2026. Why a privacy mechanism enforced by a settings toggle is not actually a privacy mechanism, and what changes when you move the check into the database row policy instead. Subscribed readers will receive the full text when it publishes.
